entry.title: SAP Developer News, March 30, 2023
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere Analytic Model Series 

  • SAP Datasphere Analytic Model Series – Motivation & Comparison with the Analytical Dataset: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/03/28/sap-datasphere-analytic-model-series-motivation-comparison-with-the-analytical-dataset/  

SAP Developer Challenges 

  • Week 4 of EDA with SAP HANA and Python: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/witalij_dataviz-eda-python-activity-7046829372317523968-sXnj/  
  • You Think You Know SAP Build? Take Our Challenge: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/03/30/you-think-you-know-sap-build-take-our-challenge/  

Updating machine types on Kyma runtime enabled 

https://blogs.sap.com/2023/03/26/updating-machine-types-on-kyma-runtime-enabled/  

SAP CodeJam Roadshow Week 3 

  • SAP CodeJam Group: https://groups.community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/gh-p/code-jam 
  • SAP CodeJam Events Calendar: https://groups.community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/eb-p/codejam-events 

SAP Developer News Podcast 

https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers 

================================================================================ 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:08 SAP Datasphere Analytic Model Series 

0:46 SAP Developer Challenges 

1:18 Updating Kyma Machine Types  

2:30 CodeJam Roadshow Week 3 

3:58 SAP Developer News Podcast

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam at Kolkata on 17th July 2023
entry.content: Thank you so much for this request.  I will work on this now with the team and be back in touch with our availability.  Josh

entry.title: 🟡 Petoberfest Day 2023!!!
entry.content:
UPDATE: This Petoberfest Fun Friday event is now officially over - as you may remember (from the description below) we actually wanted to leave this open the entire week, ending Sunday 24 September in whatever timezone you are. So as now that date has officially passed us by, we will now be counting up all the entries.  So many great pictures, thanks to everyone!

 

We look forward to all of your great submissions this year in #petoberfest 

Please share your pics of your pet (or creative representative of a "pet")  in a workplace setting of your definition here:  Click this link to the #petoberfest discussion to post your pet's picture in that comment thread and earn #devtoberfest points.

You can also find out more about this lighthearted fun day on that page.  (Comments here will be counted during week 1, but as this is a calendar entry it's not as ideal as the discussion post)

Note that this is an "all day Friday" event, whatever "Friday" means in your timezone!

entry.title: 🟡 Petoberfest Day 2023!!!
entry.content:
UPDATE: This Petoberfest Fun Friday event is now officially over - as you may remember (from the description below) we actually wanted to leave this open the entire week, ending Sunday 24 September in whatever timezone you are. So as now that date has officially passed us by, we will now be counting up all the entries.  So many great pictures, thanks to everyone!

 

Well, it's back!  THE Fun Friday event that so many of you love.  

On the first Friday of Devtoberfest this year, which will be Friday 22 September 2023, we have #Petoberfest ! 

As always, It's a lighthearted way to share your pics of pets. Yes, for those who remember, it was originally  Dogtoberfest, then we expanded to encourage pictures of all pets with Petoberfest,  A lot of you even got creative with plush animals, toys, and lots of other animal friends sharing time with you.  So to earn points in this year's 2023 #Devtoberfest, here's what you have to do:

Step 1: Take a picture of your pet in a work, coding, or general office setting (and with work from home, and mobile devices, this is a very very relaxed rule).  

Step 2: Post that picture here as a comment in this discussion post, or, if you've already posted it elsewhere, on social media or similar, share the link to that item here in the comments.

Be as creative or relaxed as you would like and have #fun , As for me, there happens to be two felines that cohabitate with me.  This is cricket expressing her usual sentiment toward me (or maybe how she feels about the CLI...):

Cricket hates the CLI

Note: We don't want to restrict you to just posting your pics here on this first Friday - you have the entire first Week 1 of Devtoberfest to post (which runs from Monday 18th of September through Sunday 24th of September). If you post outside of this period, your entry may not be credited with points in the gameboard. (Here is a link to my gameboard https://devrel-tools-prod-scn-badges-srv.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/devtoberfestContest/josh.bentley To see your gameboard, just change josh.bentley to your SAP community ID

That's it!  Happy #petoberfest

entry.title: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Gaming Night session 1 of 2
entry.content:
We are back with another fun filled game night amongst SAP friends!  A few things to note, 

  • This is session 1 of 2 happening at a more convenient time in the India time zone.
  • Join the discord server to connect and collaborate on the game you want to play or host.
  • Shrini from the Developer Advocates will be hosting a game of Poker, so if you're into that, then Shuffle up and Deal! 
  • A later session will be hosted by Thomas Jung for those in Europe and Americas.
  • And, does anyone like points for the Devtoberfest contest?  If so, go check out this community post to learn how to snag a few extra points once week 2 of Devtoberfest starts.

Please note, there is a later calendar appointment here if that works better for you

entry.title: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Gaming Night session 2 of 2
entry.content:
We are back with another fun filled game night amongst SAP friends!  A few things to note, 

  • This is session 2 of 2 happening at a more convenient time in the Europe and Americas time zones.
  • Join the discord server to connect and collaborate on the game you want to play or host.
  • Thomas Jung will be hosting a game of Ultimate Chicken Horse , I have literally no idea what that is about…
  • And, does anyone like points for the Devtoberfest contest?  If so, go check out the community post to learn how to snag a few extra points once week 2 of Devtoberfest starts. 

please note there is an alternate earlier gaming night calendar appointment here if that works better for you

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest Day 2023!!!
entry.content: Very nice!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest Day 2023!!!
entry.content: Power it on!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest Day 2023!!!
entry.content: Love this.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest Day 2023!!!
entry.content: Yes of course!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest Day 2023!!!
entry.content: And as we are almost done with week one of Devtoberfest and this year’s petoberfest I am giving you a goodbye post from JJ, not a genius this guy…

entry.title: 🟡 How Did I Miss That?
entry.content:
As a follow up to the popular session from @MichelleMoudy  last year's "How did I not see that?" We will have another great session for #FunFriday where the Developer Advocates come together to share stories of times where we were involved in a project, a go-live, a push to production, anything that really had an impact on others and we made a mistake and learned from that mistake. 

It's all part of growing and we want to have fun sharing what it is that made us who we are.  Please join us in the chat and comment, ask questions and even share your own stories if you would like.

entry.title: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Gaming Night Discussion
entry.content:
Thanks so much for attending game night and/or commenting about your favorite games.  This post is now locked from further replies. 

 

Hey everyone!  

If you are attending our game night session 1 or session 2   or if you don't attend, we wanted to provide a place besides the discord server chat where you could come and leave a comment about your experience.  What game did you play?  Did you host a game?  What was your favorite game to as a kid?  Did you have a favorite gaming console?  

Basically if you want some points for the Devtoberfest contest commenting on this thread is a super easy way to get credit for week 2 #FunFriday!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Gaming Night Discussion
entry.content: I wanted to post that my favorite game but also one that drive me nuts was the original Legend of Zelda in the gold cartridge for Nintendo Entertainment System.  It had a battery to save game progress.  I made a deal with myself that once I beat the game I would destroy the cartridge so I wouldn't have to hear that song anymore or be sucked into playing that game again.  But still, my favorite...

entry.title: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Foodtoberfest!!!
entry.content:
Thanks for participating in this Devtoberfest 2023, week 3 activity.  No new responses are being allowed now so we can tally the points earned.

 

So many great ways to earn points this year.  And now in addition to some fan favorites like Game Night and ##Petoberfest we are launching this special week 3 item:  Foodtoberfest for 500 points!

So this group is a place for you to post a picture, a recipe, a comment, anything you want on the items of food you love.  Something you snack on while coding (energy drinks and bananas come to mind).  Or maybe a family favorite you like to cook, mine is Shakshuka. (pic here, but I will post the recipe in the comments below.  Can't wait to see what everyone else post to earn their extra 500 points in the contest!

shakshuka with feta and eggs

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Foodtoberfest!!!
entry.content:
• Here is a video I made of me making shakshuka.  Shakshuka is a Northern African/Middle Eastern dish consisting of tomatoes, peppers, onions and spices. A few other items can be changed and woven into the dish.
  • Here are the ingredients and recipe:
  • 3 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil (I used Spanish)
  • 1 large onion or 2 medium onions, halved and thinly sliced
  • 1 large red bell pepper, remove stem and seeds and slice same as onion
  • 3 garlic cloves, sliced
  • 1 teaspoon ground cumin
  • 1 teaspoon paprika (not smoked, regular or sweet)
  • ⅛ teaspoon ground cayenne (red pepper)
  • 1 (28-ounce) can whole san Marzano plum tomatoes, coarsely chopped, keeping liquid from can as well
  • kosher salt (as needed, at least 1.5 teaspoons)
  • black pepper (as needed, at least 1 teaspoon)
  • 5 ounces plain crumbled feta cheese
  • 6 large eggs
  • Chopped cilantro and hot sauce for garnish (both optional)
  • Bread for dipping (like pita or naan)
  • Step 1 Heat oil in a large skillet (cast iron or anything that can go into oven later, over medium-low.
  • Step 2 Chop onion and bell pepper and add to pan. Add pinch salt and pepper to taste. Cook gently until very soft, about 20 minutes.
  • Step 3 While peppers cooking slice garlic thin and chop tomatoes into chunks.
  • Step 4. Add garlic at end of 20 minutes for about 2 additional minutes or until fragrant.
  • Step 5 Add cumin, paprika and cayenne, and cook 1 minute until all mixed into the onion/pepper/garlic mixture.
  • Step 6 heat oven to 375 Fahrenheit
  • Step 7 Pour in tomatoes and season with ¾ teaspoon salt and ¼ teaspoon pepper; taste and see if more seasoning needed. Be aware of spice from cayenne when tasting. Simmer on low to medium heat, it should be bubbling but not boiling until tomatoes have thickened, about 10 minutes. Taste and add more salt and pepper if needed. (If waiting to serve later you can stop here and even keep mixture a day or two before continuing to step 8, just reheat on stovetop and continue. Spices will actually be stronger if you do this).
  • Step 8 Stir in crumbled feta.
  • Step 9 Crack eggs into small bowl and then place into a small depression (divot) in tomatoes mixture but not touching bottom of pan. Repeat until all 6 eggs in pan, . season eggs with salt and pepper lightly. (as I mention in video, you can add meat, tofu, anything pre-cooked you want at this step instead of eggs)
  • Step 10 Transfer pan to oven and bake until eggs are just set, 8 to 10 minutes.
  • Optional Sprinkle with cilantro and serve with hot sauce. Serve with pita/naan bread if you like.

entry.title: 🟡 Fun Friday Trivia - Session 1 of 2
entry.content:
It's trivia time! 🧐

Join us for a fun quiz with questions on SAP and related topics. How will you do? How's your SAP history? Find out, and be ready to join the quiz, which we'll run twice on Friday 13 October.  One winner from each quiz will receive 500 points in the Devtoberfest contest.  To easily award points please use your SAP Community ID as your online trivia name during the session, and that ensures we can see the leader on the quiz software leaderboard in realtime:

- between 1400 and 1500 IST (this instance)

- between 1400 and 1500 EDT (the other instance)

While you'd be more than welcome to join both, the idea is that we can cover as many folks around the world as possible with these two instances. It's just a bit of fun, so if you join the second instance knowing the answers from having joined the first, you're only cheating yourself 🙂

entry.title: 🟡 Fun Friday Trivia - Session 2 of 2
entry.content:
It's trivia time! 🧐

Join us for a fun quiz with questions on SAP and related topics. How will you do? How's your SAP history? Find out, and be ready to join the quiz, which we'll run twice on Friday 13 October.  One winner from each quiz will receive 500 points in the Devtoberfest contest.  To easily award points please use your SAP Community ID as your online trivia name during the session, and that ensures we can see the leader on the quiz software leaderboard in realtime::

- between 1400 and 1500 IST (this other instance)

- between 1400 and 1500 EDT (this instance)

While you'd be more than welcome to join both, the idea is that we can cover as many folks around the world as possible with these two instances. It's just a bit of fun, so if you join the second instance knowing the answers from having joined the first, you're only cheating yourself 🙂 

 

join the quiz at 2PM EDT on youtube to watch the advocates, and the menti website to compete:  https://www.menti.com/aluj469byiby
